profile.activity_multiplier_help_html:
Your amount of calories you burn is highly dependent on how active you are.
This multiplier is used to estimate the calories burned from your daily routine.

NOTE - Do not double count workouts. If you plan to manually log workouts, do not include them in this estimate.
  • 1.1: Use 1.1 if you plan to manually log all exercise (e.g. calories burned from a daily step counter).
  • 1.2: Low activity (desk work, <5,000 steps/day)
  • 1.275: Light activity (5,000-7,500 steps/day)
  • 1.35: Moderate activity (7,500-10,000 steps/day)
  • 1.425: High activity (10,000-12,500 steps/day)
  • 1.5: Very active (15,000 steps/day))
Choose a value that best matches your typical day. This helps improve the accuracy of your weight gain/loss predictions.
profile.preferred_analyzer_help: When set, this analyzer will be automatically used when you click the camera button for food or body fat analysis.
profile.goal_help_html:
Set your goal type and daily target.

Fixed Intake: Enter the daily calorie target. Only food calories count toward your goal.

Fixed Net Calories: Enter the daily net calorie target. Food minus exercise calories count toward your goal.

Fixed Deficit: Enter the daily calorie deficit below your BMR + activity level. Your daily goal will be BMR + activity - deficit value.

Fixed Surplus: Enter the daily calorie surplus above your BMR + activity level. Your daily goal will be BMR + activity + surplus value.

Lose a fixed percent of body weight per week (Cutting):
• Enter your target weight loss percentage per week.
• Studies show that 0.5-1.0% per week is the sweet spot.
• Daily goal will then be calculated to meet your weekly weight loss goal.
• Recommend goal of 0.5-1.0% body weight per week
• Choosing more than 1.0% body weight per week will put you at high risk of losing lean body mass, which is counter productive
• Ensure you are eating enough protein and strength training to avoid muscle atrophy while cutting

Gain a fixed percent of body weight per week (Bulking):
• Enter your target weight gain percentage per week.
• Studies show that 0.25-0.5% body weight gain per week is the sweet spot.
• Daily goal will then be calculated to meet your weekly weight gain goal.
• Recommend goal of 0.25-0.5% body weight per week
• Choosing more than 0.5% body weight per week will put you at risk of gaining excess fat
profile.invalid_activity_multiplier_message: Please enter a valid activity multiplier (must be between 1.0 and 2.0).
profile.invalid_starting_weight_message: Please enter a valid starting weight (must be a positive number).
profile.invalid_goal_weight_message: Please enter a valid goal weight (must be a positive number).
profile.restart_required_message: Restart Home Assistant for changes to take effect.
profile.default_profile_set_message_html:
Default profile set to {spokenName} for HA user {userName}.
When using a voice assistant from {userName}'s companion app, {spokenName} will be the default name used when logging items (meaning you do not have to specify the user in the command. Simply "Log a cup of milk").
{spokenName} will also be the default profile loaded on the Calorie Tracker side panel when logged in as {userName}.

NOTE: A calorie tracker spoken name must still be used when logging via voice assistants not associated with a Home Assistant user, such as a Home Assistant Voice Preview Edition device.
